Trading isn’t gambling — we deliver high-accuracy calls with one priority: protect your capital first, then grow it.

You don’t need 20 trades a week.
2–3 solid wins are enough.
Overtrading is the fastest way to lose everything.

If you want real growth, you need patience.
If you want to gamble — this isn’t your place.

Success in trading comes down to 3 things:

1. Discipline


2. Patience (wait for confirmation)


3. Booking profits & accepting losses



No FOMO entries. No emotional trades.

I’m not here to scam — this is a business.
This channel runs like a modern-day brokerage with one goal: make you consistently profitable.

I know some of my HIGH TABLE members can't make money even in this setup.

So this advice for them to listen carefully

Most traders don’t fail because of strategy — they fail because of money management.

Simple example:
If you have $1000, don’t go big.
Take small trades like $10 per trade.Use 5x leverage.

Always use Stop Loss and Take Profit.
No greed, no overtrading.
Just follow this for 1 month.

You will learn: 
• how to control emotions
• how to manage risk
• how to stay consistent

Important truth: If you can manage $10 properly, you can manage $1000 or $10,000 properly too.

Start small. Learn the process. Stay disciplined.
Trading is a profession, not gambling

Trading every day isn’t real trading — it’s overtrading.

From a mathematical and strategic point of view, it doesn’t make sense. Markets need breathing space, and so do you. Even money flow isn’t constant — it cycles.

The market typically provides cleaner opportunities on

• Monday

• Tuesday

• Wednesday

The rest of the time? Often noise, manipulation, or low-probability setups.

If you call yourself a trader, then understand this:
It’s not about being in the market every day — it’s about understanding how money actually flows.

You’re here for financial freedom, not for constant action.

Protect your capital first.
Because if you can protect your money, you’re already close to becoming a successful trader.

You take an A+ setup…
Still hit SL 2–3 times in a row.

Confidence drops.
You start thinking… “Is crypto even for me?”

Relax. This phase hits every trader.

Last week?
Everything = TP 

This week?
Everything = SL 

Why?

Because the market isn’t normal right now.

 Low liquidity
 FOMC pressure
 Big players waiting on new Fed direction

That means:
Random pumps ≠ real trends
Whales are playing, not the market.

So don’t chase. Don’t overtrade.
Wait for liquidity to return.

The same market that gives SL today
Will give 100%+ tomorrow.

 Loss is part of the game
 Discipline is what keeps you alive

We wait. We strike when it’s time.

MARKET UPDATE FOR THE "HIGH TABLE"

Right now we have many active calls running. Some already hit TP, while others are still moving toward their direction. This is a normal part of the journey.

The market is currently in a mid-liquidity phase, and narratives are changing very fast. In this type of environment, patience and risk management matter more than chasing every move.

My suggestion: 
 Secure around 10% spot profit whenever possible
 Protect your capital first
 Don’t expect every trade to become a moonshot

Remember — we do NOT need to win every trade.

Out of 10 trades, even 3-5 solid winners are enough to stay profitable and grow consistently. That’s how smart traders survive long term.

We are still in a bear market structure, and during times like this, survival is more important than ego.

Protect capital. Stay disciplined. Stay patient.
The people who survive this phase are the ones who will dominate the next bull run. 

If you are stuck in any trades and need any suggestions, please  DM our analyst @tucosalamanca98
